questions.explanation: Što = what, radite = do you do (formal). It asks about someone's job or current activity.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ja ___ svaki dan.' (I work every day.)
questions.explanation: 'Radim' is 1st person singular of 'raditi' (-im conjugation). Ja radim = I work.
Čitati = to read. Pisati = to write. Gledati = to watch. Slušati = to listen.

questions.prompt: 'Ona ___ u školi.' — Choose the correct verb form. (She works at a school.)
questions.explanation: 'Radi' is 3rd person singular of 'raditi'. Ona radi = She works.

questions.prompt: What is the Croatian word for 'engineer'?
questions.explanation: Inženjer = engineer. Liječnik = doctor. Odvjetnik = lawyer. Učitelj = teacher.

questions.prompt: 'Mi ___ knjige.' — Choose the correct form. (We read books.)
questions.explanation: 'Čitamo' is 1st person plural of 'čitati' (-am conjugation). Mi čitamo = We read.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'They listen to music'?
questions.explanation: Slušati = to listen. Slušaju = they listen. Glazba = music (glazbu in accusative).
'Govoriš' is the 2nd person singular of 'govoriti' (-im conjugation).

questions.prompt: Oni ___ u uredu. (to work, 3rd person plural)
questions.explanation: 'Rade' is the 3rd person plural of 'raditi'. Note: -im verbs drop the 'i' and add -e in 3rd plural.
'Čitam' is 1st person singular of 'čitati' (-am conjugation).
'Pišete' is the 2nd person plural of 'pisati'. Note the vowel change: pis- → piš-.
'Gleda' is 3rd person singular of 'gledati' (-am conjugation).
'Igramo' is 1st person plural of 'igrati' (-am conjugation). Mi igramo = We play.
Radim = I work. U = in. Bolnica = hospital (bolnici in locative).

questions.correctAnswer: What do you do in your free time?
acceptableAnswers: What do you do in free time?
What do you do in your spare time?
questions.explanation: Slobodno vrijeme = free time. Radiš = you do (informal).
On = he, piše = writes (3rd sg of pisati), pismo = letter.
Volim = I like/love. Čitati = to read. Knjige = books (accusative plural).
Ona = she, je = is. Odvjetnica = lawyer (female form).
Na sveučilištu = at a university (locative). 'Na' is used for institutions.
Gledamo = we watch (-am conjugation). Televiziju = television (accusative).
Čita = she reads (3rd singular). Knjiga → knjigu in accusative.
Govorite = you speak (formal). 'Li' is the question particle placed after the verb.
Igra = he plays (3rd sg of igrati). Nogomet = football/soccer.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'raditi' (to work) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja radim, ti radiš, on/ona/ono radi, mi radimo, vi radite, oni/one/ona rade
questions.hint: This is a standard -im conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Raditi follows the -im pattern: -im, -iš, -i, -imo, -ite, -e. Note the 3rd person plural drops -i.
questions.exampleSentence: Radim od devet do pet svaki dan.
questions.exampleTranslation: I work from nine to five every day.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'čitati' (to read) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja čitam, ti čitaš, on/ona/ono čita, mi čitamo, vi čitate, oni/one/ona čitaju
questions.hint: This is a standard -am conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Čitati follows the -am pattern: -am, -aš, -a, -amo, -ate, -aju.
questions.exampleTranslation: I read the newspaper every morning.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'gledati' (to watch) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja gledam, ti gledaš, on/ona/ono gleda, mi gledamo, vi gledate, oni/one/ona gledaju
questions.hint: This is a standard -am conjugation verb.
questions.explanation: Gledati follows the -am pattern just like čitati.
questions.exampleTranslation: Are you watching a movie tonight?

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'pisati' (to write) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja pišem, ti pišeš, on/ona/ono piše, mi pišemo, vi pišete, oni/one/ona pišu
questions.hint: Watch out — this verb has a consonant change: s → š.
questions.explanation: Pisati has a stem change: pis- becomes piš- in all present tense forms. It follows the -em pattern.
questions.exampleTranslation: I am writing an email to a friend.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'učiti' (to learn/study) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja učim, ti učiš, on/ona/ono uči, mi učimo, vi učite, oni/one/ona uče
questions.hint: This is a standard -im conjugation verb like 'raditi'.
questions.explanation: Učiti follows the same -im pattern as raditi: -im, -iš, -i, -imo, -ite, -e.
